content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer array AAA of size NNN.

A triplet array is defined as the collection of min⁡(Ai,Aj,Ak)\min(A_i,A_j,A_k)min(Ai​,Aj​,Ak​) for all triplets (i,j,k)(i,j,k)(i,j,k), where 1≤i<j<k≤N1\le i\lt j\lt k\le N1≤i<j<k≤N.

You are given QQQ queries of the following type:


Given an integer KKK, return the value of KthK^{th}Kth **smallest** element in the triplet array.

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ — the size of array AAA and the number of queries, respectively.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots ,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The following QQQ lines describe the queries. Each of these lines contain a single positive integer KKK.




Output Format
For each query, print a single line containing the KthK^{th}Kth **smallest** element in the triplet array.
constraints: 3 ≤ N ≤ 300000 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 300000)
1 ≤ Q ≤ 300000 (sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 300000)
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and Q.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
- Each of the next Q lines contains a single integer K.
For each query output the K‑th smallest value among all min(Ai,Aj,Ak) for 1≤i<j<k≤N.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output a line with the K‑th smallest element of the triplet‑minimum array.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: All triplet minima are 3, so any K returns 3.\nTest case 2: Sorted array is [1,2,2,4]. Counts: 1 appears C(3,2)=3 times, 2 appears C(2,2)+C(1,2)=1+0=1 time, 4 appears 0 times. The sorted triplet minima are [1,1,1,2]. Queries 1,2,3,4 return 1,1,1,2 respectively.

solution.approach: Sort the array in non‑decreasing order. For the element at position i (0‑based) there are C(N‑i‑1,2) triplets whose minimum equals this element, because the other two indices must be chosen from the suffix after i. Build a prefix array pref where pref[i] = total number of triplets whose minimum is among the first i+1 elements: pref[i] = pref[i‑1] + C(N‑i‑1,2). For each query K, binary‑search the smallest index i with pref[i] ≥ K; the answer is the sorted value a[i]. All calculations fit in 64‑bit signed integers (C(300000,3) ≈ 4.5·10^15). Time complexity per test case: O(N log N + Q log N). Space complexity: O(N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + Q log N) per test case

code.Python3:
import sys

def comb2(x):
    return x * (x - 1) // 2

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); Q = int(next(it))
        a = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        a.sort()
        pref = []
        cur = 0
        for i in range(N):
            suffix = N - i - 1
            cur += comb2(suffix)
            pref.append(cur)
        for __ in range(Q):
            K = int(next(it))
            # binary search
            lo, hi = 0, N-1
            while lo < hi:
                mid = (lo + hi) // 2
                if pref[mid] >= K:
                    hi = mid
                else:
                    lo = mid + 1
            out_lines.append(str(a[lo]))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
